digest_intro: Good morning, please find below our sector-focused briefing for today, which covers 32 items of interest across the market. There are no specific updates regarding our portfolio companies in this edition.

companies.name: The Delivery Group
companies.description: UK mail, parcel and e-commerce delivery group (brands include Secured Mail, CMS Network and OnePost).

companies.name: Clearway Group
companies.description: UK vacant property and site security services group (including Clearway Labs, LociLabs and SECONTEC).

companies.name: Swytch
companies.description: UK maker of e-bike conversion kits, including the Swytch GO and Swytch MAX.

companies.name: Molinare
companies.description: London-based TV and film post-production group (Molinare Creative Group, including Notorious DIT).

companies.name: Pip Studios
companies.description: UK audio post-production and localisation studio.

companies.name: Petainer
companies.description: PET packaging manufacturer, known for petainerKeg one-way kegs.

companies.name: Whitespace
companies.description: UK sovereign AI company behind Collective / CollectiveOS.

companies.name: UOE
companies.industry: UK stationery and Post Office retail
companies.description: UK independent stationery and Post Office retail chain.

companies.name: McGraw Hill
companies.description: Global education publishing and learning technology company.

companies.name: Fertility Associates
companies.description: New Zealand fertility clinic group, with Sunfert, Sophea and IVFkasih clinics in Asia.

companies.name: Roof-Maker
companies.description: UK designer and manufacturer of rooflights.

sector_stories.title: DPD to install 270 robots to replace traditional conveyor operations
sector_stories.summary: DPD is deploying 270 autonomous robots to replace traditional conveyor belt operations within its distribution facilities.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights industry-wide shifts toward warehouse automation to improve operational efficiency and throughput in parcel processing.

sector_stories.title: Royal Mail admits massive delivery chaos is hitting dozens of UK towns today – is YOUR postcode on the dreaded list? - The Sun
sector_stories.summary: Royal Mail has acknowledged significant delivery disruptions affecting service levels across multiple locations in the United Kingdom.
sector_stories.angle: Operational failures at the dominant market incumbent often drive volume shifts toward alternative independent delivery and logistics providers.

sector_stories.title: Loganair signs term sheet with BETA Technologies for up to 10 ALIA CTOL CX300 aircraft
sector_stories.summary: Loganair has signed a term sheet to purchase up to ten all-electric ALIA CX300 aircraft, following successful demonstration flights conducted in partnership with Royal Mail.
sector_stories.angle: Demonstrates potential for electric aviation to integrate into UK mail and parcel logistics networks.

sector_stories.title: Royal Mail told changes to notifications will exclude some
sector_stories.summary: Royal Mail has introduced new 'something for you' delivery cards featuring QR codes, prompting public debate regarding digital accessibility and potential security risks from fraudulent cards.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights industry-wide challenges in balancing digital transformation with accessibility and security concerns in last-mile delivery operations.

sector_stories.title: Protecting the UK’s renewable energy infrastructure with Police CPI
sector_stories.summary: Police CPI and the National Infrastructure Crime Reduction Partnership have launched a new security guide to help protect UK solar farms from organised crime and deliberate interference.
sector_stories.angle: Provides a new industry-standard framework for security providers to align their services with national infrastructure protection requirements.

sector_stories.title: How Intelligent Video Analytics Improves Physical Security
sector_stories.summary: Intelligent video analytics technology now enables security systems to automatically detect, classify, and flag real-time threats, significantly reducing false alarms and manual monitoring requirements for security operations.
sector_stories.angle: Advances in AI-driven surveillance improve operational efficiency and threat detection capabilities for remote site and property security providers.

sector_stories.title: Access without ownership: Retail’s quiet operational risk
sector_stories.summary: Retailers are increasingly struggling with 'access drift' due to fragmented key management, leading to operational inefficiencies and security risks across large, multi-site estates.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights growing demand for centralised, technology-driven access governance and digital key management solutions within the retail property sector.

sector_stories.title: OCS reveals recommended acquisition of Mitie
sector_stories.summary: OCS has announced a recommended acquisition of Mitie to create a large, UK-headquartered international facilities management group with expanded service capabilities and geographic reach.
sector_stories.angle: Consolidation among major facilities management providers impacts the competitive landscape for integrated site security and property services.

sector_stories.title: The e - bike boom : Electric bicycles are giving riders new freedom while fueling safety concerns
sector_stories.summary: Massachusetts is considering the 'Ride Safe Act' to establish statewide e-bike regulations based on speed, amid rising concerns over safety, battery-related fires, and increased injury rates.
sector_stories.angle: Regulatory scrutiny and safety concerns regarding battery standards and speed limits are intensifying across key micromobility markets.

sector_stories.title: AAA Launches E - Ride Safety Campaign as E - Bike , E - Scooter Use Grows
sector_stories.summary: AAA has launched an E-Ride Safety campaign in response to rising injury rates and a lack of safety awareness regarding e-bikes and e-scooters.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights growing regulatory and public safety scrutiny, which may influence future product standards and consumer safety expectations.

sector_stories.title: Orange County School Board to discuss e - bikes , AI policy
sector_stories.summary: The Orange County School Board is considering a policy to restrict e-bike usage on school campuses, including potential bans for students without a driver's license.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights growing regulatory scrutiny and safety concerns regarding e-bike usage among younger demographics in public spaces.

sector_stories.title: Lime dismantled a scooter before police could inspect it after a fatal Auckland crash , coroner finds
sector_stories.summary: A New Zealand coroner has recommended that e-scooters involved in fatal crashes be preserved for police inspection after Lime dismantled a vehicle following a 2019 incident.
sector_stories.angle: Regulatory scrutiny regarding post-accident evidence preservation and safety standards for shared micromobility fleets is increasing.

sector_stories.title: Autodesk Adds AI and 3DGS Tools Across Maya, 3ds Max and More
sector_stories.summary: Autodesk has introduced new AI-driven motion tools, editable 3D Gaussian splatting, and enhanced collaborative review features across its Maya, 3ds Max, and Flow Production Tracking software suites.
sector_stories.angle: These updates directly impact post-production efficiency, animation workflows, and remote collaboration capabilities for visual effects studios.

sector_stories.title: Chaos V-Ray 7, Update 4: Final, Real-Time Rendering in Viewport, Deeper VP Integration
sector_stories.summary: Chaos has released V-Ray 7, Update 4, introducing real-time viewport rendering, Gaussian splat relighting, and enhanced virtual production tools to streamline post-production and on-set workflows.
sector_stories.angle: Advancements in real-time rendering and virtual production integration significantly improve pipeline efficiency and creative control for post-production studios.

sector_stories.title: Foundry Launches SmartRoto AI Plugin for Nuke
sector_stories.summary: Foundry has launched SmartRoto, an AI-powered plugin for Nuke designed to automate rotoscoping tasks and improve artist efficiency by up to four times.
sector_stories.angle: Adoption of AI-driven automation tools is essential for maintaining competitiveness and managing increasing VFX shot volumes and complexity.

sector_stories.title: Sohonet and Bind Studio Expand Partnership to Deliver Turnkey VFX
sector_stories.summary: Sohonet and Bind Studio have expanded their partnership to provide a turnkey VFX production platform, integrating infrastructure, storage, and review tools into a single managed service.
sector_stories.angle: Trend towards cloud-based, turnkey infrastructure solutions reducing the operational burden of managing complex VFX pipelines for productions.

sector_stories.title: Angela Eagle becomes Defra secretary as Burnham unveils cabinet
sector_stories.summary: Angela Eagle has been appointed Defra secretary under Prime Minister Andy Burnham, overseeing critical recycling and waste reforms alongside environmental targets.
sector_stories.angle: Defra's leadership changes impact the implementation of UK recycling reforms and circular economy policies affecting PET packaging.

sector_stories.title: UK faces £750m gap in bottle recycling infrastructure: Enviroo
sector_stories.summary: Enviroo reports a £750m infrastructure gap in UK PET recycling capacity, highlighting that current food-grade pelletisation facilities are insufficient to handle volumes expected from the 2027 deposit return scheme.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights critical supply chain bottlenecks and regulatory shifts impacting the availability and cost of recycled PET material.

sector_stories.title: Carbios reaches milestone of 100 batches at its Industrial demonstration plant and expands its licensing offering to the textile market
sector_stories.summary: Carbios has successfully processed 100 batches at its industrial demonstration plant and is expanding its enzymatic PET biorecycling licensing to the global textile market.
sector_stories.angle: Advances in enzymatic recycling technology for PET increase the potential availability of high-quality recycled monomers for packaging manufacturers.

sector_stories.title: India targets 60 % recycled content in rigid packaging
sector_stories.summary: India has introduced an Extended Producer Responsibility framework requiring rigid plastic packaging to contain at least 60% recycled content by the 2028–29 financial year.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights global regulatory trends mandating recycled content, impacting material sourcing and circular design strategies for packaging manufacturers.

sector_stories.title: Samsung in talks to back France Mistral at €20bn valuation
sector_stories.summary: Mistral is in advanced talks to raise capital at a €20bn valuation, with potential backing from Samsung, EQT, and other major investors to scale European sovereign AI infrastructure.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights significant capital inflows and market validation for European sovereign AI alternatives to US-controlled cloud models.

sector_stories.title: Vodafone Business Joins Cosine Coalition to Develop united kingdom Sovereign AI Model for Critical Sectors
sector_stories.summary: Vodafone Business has joined the Cosine coalition to co-design Lumen Sovereign, a UK-based frontier AI model intended for critical sectors and regulated industries.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights increasing market demand for sovereign AI infrastructure and domestic collaboration in the UK's regulated enterprise sector.

sector_stories.title: House of Lords questions Andy Burnham AI plans
sector_stories.summary: The UK government is restructuring science and technology departments, moving the Sovereign AI Unit to the new Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights institutional shifts and policy continuity for UK sovereign AI infrastructure and government funding priorities.

sector_stories.title: Microsoft and Mistral Expand AI Partnership with Multibillion - Dollar Europe Compute Investment
sector_stories.summary: Microsoft and Mistral have expanded their partnership to increase European AI compute capacity and provide flexible deployment options, including air-gapped environments for regulated industries.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights growing market demand for sovereign AI infrastructure and secure, compliant deployment models within the European enterprise sector.

sector_stories.industry: UK stationery and Post Office retail
sector_stories.title: ACS responds to Treasury call for evidence on in-person banking services
sector_stories.summary: The Association of Convenience Stores has urged the Treasury to mitigate business costs to ensure the continued viability of essential in-person banking and cash services in retail outlets.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights the critical role of retail-based banking services and the regulatory pressure to maintain their economic viability.

sector_stories.industry: UK stationery and Post Office retail
sector_stories.title: Falling retail crime stats received with very cautious optimism
sector_stories.summary: Official statistics show recorded shop theft in England and Wales fell to 507,086 offences in the year to March 2026, though industry bodies warn underreporting remains a significant issue.
sector_stories.angle: Retailers face ongoing operational costs and safety challenges despite slight improvements in official crime statistics.

sector_stories.industry: UK stationery and Post Office retail
sector_stories.title: Independent retail associations react to PM’s business rates announcement
sector_stories.summary: Prime Minister Andy Burnham announced a 20% business rates discount for pubs and music venues, with further reforms to Small Business Rates Relief promised for the upcoming Budget.
sector_stories.angle: Signals potential future business rates reform and highlights ongoing government focus on high street retail support.

sector_stories.industry: UK stationery and Post Office retail
sector_stories.title: Yoti and Licensing Connect help premises prepare for digital age verification checks
sector_stories.summary: Yoti and Licensing Connect have partnered to enable retail businesses to use digital IDs for age verification, ahead of upcoming changes to Mandatory Licensing Conditions in Autumn 2026.
sector_stories.angle: Provides a new digital compliance tool for retailers selling age-restricted goods, reducing reliance on physical ID checks.

sector_stories.title: U.K. Prime Minister Axes Science Department, Names New Education Secretary
sector_stories.summary: Prime Minister Andy Burnham has appointed Lucy Powell as education secretary and merged the Department of Business and Trade with the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology.
sector_stories.angle: New leadership and departmental restructuring signal potential shifts in UK higher education funding and national skills policy priorities.

sector_stories.title: A Call to Meet the Moment at NACUBO
sector_stories.summary: The 2026 NACUBO annual meeting highlighted institutional concerns regarding federal policy changes, student loan restrictions, and the cautious, experimental adoption of artificial intelligence in higher education administration.
sector_stories.angle: Provides critical insight into higher education budget priorities, regulatory headwinds, and institutional technology adoption trends.

sector_stories.title: New Push to Fix Calculus Bottleneck
sector_stories.summary: Every Learner Everywhere has launched a department-wide initiative across 12 institutions to redesign calculus instruction using AI-enabled courseware and data-driven continuous improvement strategies.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights a shift toward department-wide course redesign and free, AI-enabled courseware as alternatives to traditional textbook models.

sector_stories.title: AAUP Denounces Politician, Donor ‘Extortion’ of Universities
sector_stories.summary: The AAUP has released a report alleging that political and donor pressure is threatening institutional autonomy, academic freedom, and shared governance within higher education institutions.
sector_stories.angle: Increased political intervention in university governance and curriculum design impacts the operational environment for higher education publishers.

sector_stories.title: Pro - Life Group Wants More Ethical Limits and Regulations on IVF
sector_stories.summary: Liberty Counsel Action has formally urged the U.S. government to prioritize Restorative Reproductive Medicine over IVF coverage in proposed federal health insurance rules.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights increasing political and regulatory pressure on the IVF industry regarding ethical standards and insurance coverage frameworks.

sector_stories.title: Beyond awareness: Why we need a top-down approach to fertility education
sector_stories.summary: Medical experts are calling for improved fertility education and evidence-based clinical advice to address patient misconceptions regarding AMH testing, egg freezing, and reproductive conditions like PMOS.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights the critical need for clinical expertise to balance the rise of direct-to-consumer femtech and diagnostic testing.

sector_stories.title: Human sperm precursors grown from stem cells in lab
sector_stories.summary: Researchers at the University of Pennsylvania have successfully developed immature human sperm cells from blood-derived stem cells using a mouse-based artificial testicle model.
sector_stories.angle: Advances in in vitro gametogenesis represent long-term disruptive potential for treating male factor infertility and regulatory frameworks.

sector_stories.title: Surrogacy sperm mix-up means intended parents must adopt twins
sector_stories.summary: A UK court ruled that intended parents must adopt twins born via surrogacy in Sri Lanka after a clinic used donor sperm instead of the father's.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights critical risks regarding clinical governance, chain of custody, and the legal implications of gamete mix-ups in IVF.

sector_stories.title: Kier and Bam appointed to Crown Estate West End schemes
sector_stories.summary: The Crown Estate has appointed Kier and BAM to deliver two major mixed-use redevelopment projects in London’s West End, focusing on high sustainability and environmental standards.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights demand for high-specification commercial retrofits and stringent sustainability certifications in the London construction market.

sector_stories.title: Morgan Sindall revenue hits £2.56bn in record first half
sector_stories.summary: Morgan Sindall reported record first-half revenue of £2.56bn, reflecting strong activity levels within the UK construction sector.
sector_stories.angle: Provides a key indicator of market health and demand levels for major contractors within the UK construction industry.

sector_stories.title: Sir Robert McAlpine in high-rise fire safety defects claim
sector_stories.summary: Sir Robert McAlpine faces a £1.2m damages claim over alleged fire safety defects in a residential development, leading to extensive remediation works.
sector_stories.angle: Highlights increasing legal and regulatory scrutiny regarding fire safety compliance and building standards in the UK construction sector.

sector_stories.title: McDonald backed to retain construction brief under Burnham
sector_stories.summary: Chris McDonald is expected to retain the construction minister brief within the newly formed Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade under Prime Minister Andy Burnham.
sector_stories.angle: Continuity in ministerial leadership provides stability for construction policy, regulatory frameworks, and long-term infrastructure investment planning.
